entry.title: Building a Foundation for Smarter Integrations with SAP Integration Suite and Databricks.
entry.content:
As businesses increasingly rely on cloud-based data platforms, seamless integration between SAP and Databricks is essential for organizations looking to leverage their enterprise data effectively. SAP systems house vast amounts of transactional data, while Databricks offers a powerful platform for processing and analyzing large datasets. However, integrating these two environments comes with challenges such as data transformation, large-scale processing, and efficient synchronization.

Recent developments, including SAP's expanded partnership with Databricks, have highlighted the growing need for better data interoperability. As enterprises seek to unify SAP data with modern analytics and processing platforms, having a reliable and optimized integration strategy becomes crucial.

The Advantco Databricks adapter can simplify the integration process by addressing key technical limitations, enabling businesses to process data efficiently, optimize query execution, and maintain real-time synchronization between SAP and Databricks environments.

Challenges in SAP-Databricks Integration

While SAP Integration Suite provides a framework for external integrations, connecting SAP data with Databricks efficiently requires overcoming several technical obstacles:

  • Complex Data Transformation – SAP formats (IDocs, BAPIs) need to be converted into SQL-based structures compatible with Databricks.
  • Handling Large Data Volumes – The Databricks API enforces a 16 MiB limit on SQL statement sizes, requiring data chunking and optimization.
  • Query Execution and Performance – Extracting large datasets demands automated pagination and incremental data retrieval to avoid performance bottlenecks.
  • Real-Time Data Synchronization – Many organizations require up-to-date data flows between SAP and Databricks, but SAP Integration Suite does not natively support real-time Change Data Capture (CDC).
  • Schema and Connection Management – Without automated schema management tools, integrating evolving datasets between the two platforms becomes increasingly complex.

Optimizing SAP-Databricks Integration with a Dedicated Adapter

1. Efficient Data Transformation with SQL Statements

SAP data is structured differently from cloud data platforms like Databricks, making data transformation a critical step in the integration process. A well-designed Databricks adapter simplifies this challenge by:

  • Supporting Built-in SQL Execution – Allowing direct Insert, Update, Delete, and Select operations within Databricks.
  • Handling Nested Data Structures – Ensuring seamless integration of hierarchical SAP data, such as Arrays, Maps, and Structs.
  • Executing Multiple SQL Statements in a Single Payload – Reducing latency and improving efficiency for batch processing.
  • Converting SAP Data Formats – Adapting IDocs and BAPIs into tabular structures that align with Databricks’ data processing capabilities.

These capabilities reduce manual transformation efforts and streamline the integration of SAP data into Databricks workflows.

Figure 1: Insert XML Statement with Nested Structure

2. Managing Large Data Transfers with Intelligent Chunking

SAP transaction volumes can be extensive, making it challenging to transfer data without hitting API limitations or performance bottlenecks. A Databricks adapter optimizes large-scale data handling through:

  • Automatic Message Chunking – Breaking down large datasets into manageable pieces while preserving transaction integrity.
  • Parallel Processing – Enhancing data ingestion speeds by processing multiple chunks simultaneously.
  • Error Handling and Retries – Ensuring seamless data transfers by automatically retrying failed transactions.
  • Batch Execution for High-Volume Processing – Allowing bulk data ingestion without exceeding Databricks’ API constraints.

These optimizations ensure that SAP-to-Databricks data pipelines remain scalable, reliable, and high-performing.

3. Optimized Query Execution and Pagination for Large Datasets

To efficiently retrieve and process large datasets from SAP in Databricks, an integration solution should support:

  • Incremental Data Retrieval – Fetching only new and updated records instead of full dataset refreshes.
  • Automated Pagination – Breaking down large query results into smaller parts to prevent timeouts.
  • Configurable Query Modes – Supporting scheduled, real-time, and batch data extractions for operational flexibility.
  • Optimized Query Execution – Filtering and pre-sorting records at the database level to minimize unnecessary data transfers.

With intelligent query handling, businesses can accelerate data access while minimizing resource consumption.

Figure 2: Pagination-Included Query Parameters

4. Real-Time Change Data Capture (CDC) for Synchronization

Keeping SAP and Databricks environments synchronized is essential for organizations relying on up-to-date insights. A Databricks adapter enhances CDC by:

  • Converting Parquet to XML/JSON – Ensuring compatibility with SAP’s expected data formats.
  • Tracking Inserts, Updates, and Deletes – Keeping both systems aligned with minimal manual intervention.
  • Streaming CDC Events – Enabling near real-time updates for faster decision-making.
  • Filtering and Processing Only Relevant Changes – Reducing unnecessary data loads and improving system efficiency.

With real-time CDC support, businesses can ensure that Databricks always has the latest SAP data available for analytics and reporting.

Figure 3: CDC Configuration with Response in XML format

5. Simplifying Connection and Schema Management

Establishing and maintaining reliable connections between SAP and Databricks can be complex without dedicated tools. The Advantco Databricks adapter simplifies this by offering:

  • Automated Schema Exploration – Allowing users to browse Databricks tables, views, and metadata.
  • Auto-Generated XML and XSLT Payloads – Reducing manual effort in defining data mappings and transformations.
  • Seamless Unity Catalog Integration – Ensuring governed and secure access to enterprise datasets.
  • Built-in Connection Health Monitoring – Detecting and resolving potential integration failures proactively.

With these capabilities, organizations can reduce manual configuration efforts and improve overall data governance.

Figure 4: Catalog Explorer for Generate Table Schemas

Conclusion: Laying the Bricks for a Future-Ready Enterprise Integration

The Advantco Databricks adapter extends SAP Integration Suite capabilities by enabling:

  • Seamless Data Transformation – Converting SAP-specific formats into structures optimized for Databricks.
  • Efficient Data Handling – Managing large data volumes through chunking and parallel processing.
  • Optimized Query Execution – Retrieving large datasets efficiently with pagination and filtering.
  • Real-Time Synchronization – Keeping SAP and Databricks aligned with CDC-driven updates.
  • Automated Schema and Connection Management – Reducing manual effort while improving data governance.

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2502/SolP/1KA?region=US

Jerry

entry.title: Mastering the Implementation of SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub: Lessons Learned and Best
entry.content:
Implementing SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) is a strategic move that enables organisations to transition toward skills-based talent management. However, this transformation is not without its challenges. From ensuring data readiness to integrating with existing HR systems, organisations must navigate several complexities to achieve a successful implementation. Based on real-world experiences, here are the key lessons learned and best practices to consider when implementing TIH.

Start with a Strong Foundation

One of the most common pitfalls in implementing TIH is underestimating the importance of a well-structured foundation. Before enabling TIH, organisations must assess their existing skills infrastructure to ensure their data is accurate, structured, and aligned with business needs. Many organisations have unstructured or outdated skills data spread across multiple systems, making establishing a single source of truth difficult. It is essential to thoroughly audit existing skills, competencies, and job profiles to determine what can be migrated and what needs to be refined.

Organisations using Job Profile Builder (JPB) must complete the migration from Job Description Manager (JDM) to JPB before moving to TIH. Failure to do so can lead to inconsistencies and potential data loss. Also, I would like to point out that defining a precise proficiency rating scale early in the process is essential. Organisations may struggle to assess employee skills and make informed talent decisions without standardised proficiency levels. Having a clear framework will ensure consistency across the system and improve the accuracy of skills-based decision-making.

Leverage AI, but Keep a Human Touch

Introducing AI-assisted skills inference in TIH is a significant advancement in automating skills identification and recommendations. However, AI alone cannot replace human validation and expertise. AI-driven recommendations are only as good as the underlying data, which means that well-defined job descriptions and role expectations are crucial for ensuring high-quality results. Organisations should involve managers and HR professionals early in the process to validate AI-generated skills and ensure that they align with actual job requirements.

While AI can suggest relevant skills based on an employee’s experience and learning history, it is essential to provide employees with the ability to refine and customise their own skills profiles. Employees should be encouraged to manage their Growth Portfolio actively, updating their skills based on their career aspirations and learning progress. This hybrid approach—combining AI-driven recommendations with human oversight—ensures a more accurate and personalised skills framework.

Integration is Key

One of TIH's most powerful aspects is its ability to integrate seamlessly with other SAP SuccessFactors modules. However, organisations often underestimate the effort required to align these systems. For instance, integrating TIH with SAP SuccessFactors Learning allows organisations to use AI-assisted skills tagging to recommend learning courses tailored to individual skill gaps. This integration ensures employees receive targeted upskilling opportunities, making learning more relevant and practical

Similarly, integrating TIH with #Career and Talent Development enables employees to track their skill progression and discover internal opportunities that match their strengths. Organisations leveraging these integrations can create a dynamic talent ecosystem where employees continuously grow and advance in their careers. Aligning TIH with recruiting processes is another critical step, as it allows HR teams to match internal talent with open positions based on skills, reducing external hiring costs and improving workforce agility

Plan Your Migration Carefully

Migrating to TIH is not a simple technical upgrade—it requires careful planning and execution. Before initiating the migration, organisations should start by running system readiness checks using the SAP Check Tool to identify and resolve data inconsistencies. Common issues include duplicate competencies, missing proficiency ratings, and outdated role definitions. Addressing these problems in advance helps prevent migration failures and reduces the risk of data corruption.

Before moving to a live environment, you should conduct a test migration in a sandbox instance. This way, HR teams can validate data integrity, identify potential issues, and refine configurations without disrupting ongoing business operations. Additionally, a well-structured change management strategy is essential. HR teams and employees must be trained to use TIH effectively, including managing their Growth Portfolio, interpreting AI-driven recommendations, and utilising integrated learning and career development features. Clear communication and hands-on training ensure a smoother transition and higher adoption rates.

Make Skills Management a Continuous Process

Implementing TIH is not a one-time event but an ongoing process requiring continuous refinement and optimisation. Organisations should establish mechanisms to encourage employees to update their Growth portfolios regularly. By doing so, employees take ownership of their skills development, ensuring their profiles remain accurate and reflect their evolving capabilities.

Regular data reviews and governance processes should also be implemented to ensure that job profiles, skills taxonomies, and proficiency levels align with industry trends and business needs. Without ongoing maintenance, skills data can quickly become outdated, diminishing the value of the TIH implementation. HR teams should leverage analytics and reporting tools within TIH to monitor adoption rates, track skills coverage across the organisation, and identify emerging skills gaps. This data-driven approach allows organisations to make informed decisions about workforce planning, learning investments, and internal mobility strategies.

A successful TIH implementation requires a well-thought-out strategy that balances technical execution with organisational change management. Organisations that take the time to build a strong foundation, integrate TIH with key HR processes, and continuously refine their skills framework will be best positioned to realise the full potential of skills-based talent management. More than just a system upgrade, TIH represents a fundamental shift in how organisations understand, develop, and utilise their workforce. Those who embrace this transformation strategically will gain a competitive advantage in building a future-ready workforce.

 

As organisations continue to shift towards skills-based talent management, the success of Talent Intelligence Hub depends not just on its implementation but on how well it is adopted and continuously refined. What has been your biggest challenge or insight when transitioning to a skills-based approach?

entry.title: Your Path to SAP Integration Suite Certification: Courses, Exam Prep, and Expert Tips
entry.content:
Certifications are a great way to upskill and validate your expertise. If you are a developer and work within the integration space, the key certification to consider is the SAP Certified Associate - Integration Developer. This guide will tell you more about this exam and what to do to prepare.  

 

About the Exam 

Information about the exams, including the number of questions, cut scores and sample questions are published on learning.sap.com/certifications. The recommended certification for developers working in the integration space is the SAP Certified Associate - Integration Developer.  

The certification website gives you a breakdown of topic areas covered in the exam and their weight, so you know what to focus on. Check the exam page for the most up to date information. 

The certifications are valid for 12 months. You can extend the certification by 12 more months by completing an assessment. You will receive a heads up from SAP to let you know when it’s time to renew your certification. 

 

How to Schedule the Exam 

You can schedule your SAP Integration Suite certification exam in two ways: 

If You Have a Learning Hub Subscription 

With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you receive four exam attempts per year as part of your plan. You can use these attempts to take up to four different certification exams or to retake an exam if needed. 

To schedule your exam: 

  1. Log in to learning.sap.com. 
  2. Find the certification exam you want to take. 
  3. Click Schedule. This will take you to the Questionmark certification portal, where you can view and manage your exam dates. 
  4. On the Questionmark certification portal, click Exam dashboard from the top-level navigation. Then click Schedule an exam.  
  5. Find the certification exam you want to take from the list (there are a lot, and it’s easier to do a control / command + F). Click the calendar icon to select the desired date and time. Then click Save Appointment. 
  6. To view your scheduled exams, click Exam Appointments from the top-level navigation.  

If You Don’t Have a Learning Hub Subscription 

If you don’t have a Learning Hub subscription, you can purchase a set of certification attempts.  

You have three purchase options: 

  • One certification attempt, available to purchase here. 
  • Two certification attempts, including 10 hours of practice system access with it, available to purchase here. 
  • A bundle of six attempts (usable for different certifications or exam retakes if needed), available to purchase here. 

Once purchased, follow the scheduling instructions on the website to book your exam. 

 

How to Stay Certified 

The SAP Integration Suite certification is valid for one year. To maintain your certification, you’ll need to renew it annually. 

  • If you have a Learning Hub subscription: You can renew your certification by completing a short assessment covering the latest SAP Integration Suite innovations. 
  • If you don’t have a Learning Hub subscription: You must purchase a certification attempt and retake the full exam. You can do so here. 

For more details, visit the SAP certification renewal guide. 

 

What to Study for the SAP Integration Suite Certification: Learning Paths  

The best way to prepare for the exam is to take learning paths. Completing the learning paths along with their quizzes and hands on exercises will provide the knowledge and practice needed to pass the certification. Below are the recommended learning paths to pass the SAP Integration Suite certification exam: 

  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
  • Developing with SAP Integration Suite learning path  

The learning paths take about 12 hours to complete, and they cover all relevant topics you need to pass the exam.  

 

Additional Resources to Support Your Learning 

While the learning paths cover all the important topics required to pass your certification exam, it’s always helpful to reinforce your learning with additional resources and hands-on practice. Here are some valuable materials to deepen your understanding of SAP Integration Suite and boost your confidence before the exam. 

Live Sessions – SAP Build 

Join expert-led live sessions on SAP Integration Suite to get hands-on guidance, ask questions, and stay updated on the latest developments. See the upcoming live sessions here. You need the Learning Hub subscription to access these sessions. 

SAP CodeJams 

Participate in free CodeJam sessions, where you can practice SAP Integration Suite in a collaborative, hands-on environment. These sessions are ideal for gaining real-world experience and troubleshooting with experts. More info here. 

 

SAP Integration Suite Missions & Tutorials 

Explore interactive missions and tutorials on the SAP Developers site. These step-by-step guides will help you apply what you’ve learned and build confidence before taking the certification exam. 

 

Your SAP Integration Suite Exam Prep Checklist 

Follow these steps to maximize your chances of passing the exam on your first attempt. 

  1. Understand the exam structure.  
    • Review the certification webpage to learn about the exam topics and their weight, so you know where to focus your studies. 
    • Familiarize yourself with the exam format, including the number of questions, time limit, and passing score. 
  2. Follow the recommended learning journey 
    • Complete all the recommended learning paths outlined in this post. 
    • Make sure to complete all hands-on activities and quizzes to reinforce your understanding. 
    • Alternatively, attend a classroom training to prepare for the certification. There, you will get access to a real system to complete exercises on your own. See the training page for upcoming classroom sessions. 
  3. Get more hands-on experience 
    • Practice using SAP Integration Suite in different scenarios. For example, you can complete missions in the discovery center.   
    • Experiment with SAP Integration Suite features beyond the course exercises to deepen your understanding. 
  4. Create a study plan 
    • Set a realistic study schedule. Spread your learning over a few weeks rather than cramming at the last minute. 
  5. Take a Practice Test  
    • Use the sample questions provided by SAP to get a feel for the exam format and question style. 
  6. Rest well before the exam 
    • Avoid last-minute cramming. It’s better to be well-rested and focused. 
  7. Read Questions Carefully During the Exam 
    • Pay attention to keywords like "best," "most effective," or "first step." 
    • Eliminate obviously wrong answers before choosing the best one. 
    • If unsure, mark the question for review and return to it later. 

 

Share Your Certification Journey and Help Others Succeed 

Earning your SAP Integration Suite certification is a great achievement, and your experience can help others on the same path. Share your journey in the Integration Group by posting about your study strategies, favorite resources, and exam tips. Whether it’s a learning method that worked well for you or a resource you found particularly useful, your insights could make a big difference for those preparing for the exam.

entry.title: Allowance Benefit - Enrollment workflow approval not creating pay component assignment
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have an allowance for which employees can self-enroll. The enrollment needs to be reviewed and approved by Benefits admin and then the allowance pay component should be added to the comp info portlet.

First we tested this allowance without having the Benefit enrollment workflow. When the employee self-enrolls for the allowance and there is no workflow, we see that an event Reason PCAssignment gets created on the employee's comp info and the allowance pay component is added.

In the 2nd test (which is the actual requirement),  we created the business rule in screenshot and added it as a Save Rule in the "Benefits Enrollment" object. On adding the business rule we saw that when employee enrolls in the allowance, the workflow got triggered to the Benefits admin and benefits admin approved the workflow. However, once the workflow is approved we didn’t see the event Reason PCAssignment getting created on the employee's comp info and the allowance pay component did not got added.

If you have implemented such a requirement or have inputs regarding how to implement, please let us know.

Regards,

Rahul

P.S: The benefit admin had the permission to add the allowance pay component on the employee’s comp info.

entry.title: Roadmap for migration of SAP BO artifacts to SAP SAC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

This question pertains to the possibility of SAP BO artifacts (reports & dashboards) to be migrated  to SAP SAC. My understanding from reading the other blogs(2 yrs old) is that there is no migration tool provided by SAP to migrate BO report to SAC. What's the direction from SAP for clients who are heavily invested in SAP BO planning to move to SAC?

We currently have 400+ BO report/dashboards that need to be migrated to SAC. Considering the complexity this is a lot of manual effort. We are planning to go with Rise with SAP to migrate the current on-premises environment to the cloud. Until now, there is no product roadmap for migrating existing SAP BO WEBI Reports to SAC.

Wanted to understand the available options before we explore any further.

entry.title: Solution Manager - Application monitoring for 'Deactivated Update'
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We used to have a situation where our update gets deactivated (SM14) for an application server as per SAP note - DIGITS - Update has been deactivated sporadically in SM13 or SM14.

We would like to know if there is an alert metrics with solution manager to capture this event and get an alert.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solution Manager - Application monitoring for 'Deacti...
entry.content:
From system monitoring side, we don't have standard template to monitoring on the SM13 & SM14 deactivated updates. 

In SAP standard template, we only have below two metrics that are relevant for update:

Number of updates in error status (older 1 day)

Update Status  -- Info Only

KBA below is for your information:
2774008 - How to monitor the number of update process in error status of last 24 hours 

Since there's no standard metrics available for your need,  you would need to refer to KBA ##2484872 to create your custom metrics. However, creating custom metrics might need the involvement of your development teams and would be fall into consulting issue. 

##2484872 - Requested metric not included in standard templates

To help you could check and review the following wiki link on the development of custom data providers:

https://wiki.scn.sap.com/wiki/x/TgGEFQ

If you require assistance from SAP development to help with the custom development this would be at a chargeable cost, there is more information in the following Note which includes all the relevant links:

11 - Would you like to shape & influence future releases of SAP Products?

entry.title: Issue Regarding Querying the Actual Payment Amount for Purchase Orders
entry.content: 查询采购订单实际付款金额的问题 Issue Regarding Querying the Actual Payment Amount for Purchase Orders （System: ERP6.0, ECC EHP8）实际业务中用户有查询采购订单实际付款金额的需求。已开发报表通过取已清表bsak中采购订单字段的数据来取已付金额，即发票校验环节应付行项目采购订单字段。但是该字段数据存在问题，导致已付金额取值不准，该字段的详细问题过程如下： In actual business operations, users have a requirement to query the actual payment amount for purchase orders. A custom report was developed to retrieve the paid amount by extracting data from the purchase order field in the cleared items table BSAK, which corresponds to the purchase order field in the payable line items during the invoice verification process. However, there are issues with the data in this field, leading to inaccurate retrieval of the paid amount. The detailed issues with this field are outlined below: 1、系统刚上线时。发票校验环节，生成的会计凭证中应付行项目的采购订单字段为空。 1. During the initial system go-live phase, in the invoice verification process, the purchase order field in the payable line items of the generated accounting documents was empty. 2、2021年6月，关于发票校验环节采购订单字段的多个功能增强传输到生产系统，此处涉及以下两个功能增强点： 2. In June 2021, multiple functional enhancements related to the purchase order fields in the invoice verification process were transported to the production system. This involved the following two enhancement points: （1）功能增强点一：为发票校验会计凭证的应付行项目的采购订单字段赋值 (1) Enhancement Point 1: Populating the purchase order field in the payable line items of accounting documents generated during invoice verification. ①部分程序增强内容如下： ①Enhancements to some programs include the following: ②该程序增强的业务逻辑是应付行项目的采购订单字段取采购凭证中的其他行的采购订单字段，如下图： ②The enhanced business logic of this program is that the purchase order field in the accounts payable line item retrieves the purchase order fields from other lines within the purchase document, as shown in the figure below: ③该方式存在的问题是：若一张发票校验凭证涉及多笔采购订单，生成的会计凭证的供应商挂账会将多笔采购订单进行合并。导致应付行项目的金额是多笔订单的合计应付金额，而应付行项目的采购订单字段是发票校验涉及的其中一个采购订单号。如下图所示： ③The issue with this method is: If an invoice verification document involves multiple purchase orders, the generated accounting document will consolidate the vendor liabilities across those purchase orders. This results in the accounts payable line item amount reflecting the total payable sum of multiple orders, while the purchase order field in the payable line item only displays one of the involved purchase order numbers. As shown in the example below: （2）功能增强点二：在金额分摊页签新增了采购订单字段 (2) Functional Enhancement 2: Added the Purchase Order field in the Amount Distribution tab ①部分程序增强内容如下： ①Partial program enhancements are as follows: ②该程序增强的业务逻辑是通过金额分摊页签录入不同的采购订单的金额 ②The enhanced business logic of the program allows entering amounts for different purchase orders through the Amount Allocation tab. ③存在的问题是：回车后采购订单字段被强制清空，生成的会计凭证的应付行项目的采购订单字段仍为空。 ③The existing issues are: after pressing Enter, the purchase order field is forcibly cleared, and the purchase order field in the accounts payable (AP) line item of the generated accounting document remains empty. ④总结：这个采购订单会被清空的问题，通过修改增强程序能够解决，当时的业务顾问想验证一下后续的付款操作是否会出现问题。尝试在系统里直接写入EBELN数据进行测试，发现即使这一步操作后，BSEG里的EBELN有值，但是付款时仍然会被合并。所以该功能增强未进一步推进。 ④Summary: The issue where the purchase order (PO) field is cleared can be resolved by modifying the enhanced program. However, the business consultant at the time wanted to verify whether subsequent payment operations would encounter problems. Testing was conducted by directly writing EBELN (PO number) data into the system. It was discovered that even after this step, although the EBELN field in BSEG (accounting document line items) contained a value, payments were still consolidated during processing. As a result, further development of this enhancement was halted. 基于以上原因，已开发报表中的采购订单实际付款金额并不准确。希望SAP团队提供技术支持解决该问题，或其他方式取到采购订单的实际已付金额。 Based on the above reason/description, the actual payment amounts for purchase orders in the developed report are currently inaccurate. We request technical support from the SAP team to resolve this issue or provide alternative methods to accurately retrieve the actual paid amounts for purchase orders.

entry.title: 探索SAP扩展架构指南：开启企业定制化之路
entry.content:
在当今快速变化的商业环境中，企业需要灵活应对各种挑战和机遇。为了实现业务流程的定制化和扩展，SAP扩展架构指南（Extension Architecture Guide）为企业提供了一个决策框架。然而，许多企业在选择合适的SAP技术时面临困惑，不知道如何在不同场景下做出最佳决策。缺乏清晰的指导可能导致资源浪费和效率低下。那么，SAP扩展架构指南如何帮助企业在复杂的技术环境中做出明智的决策，实现业务流程的定制化和扩展呢？

什么是SAP扩展架构指南？

SAP扩展架构指南是SAP BTP指导框架的一部分，旨在帮助企业架构师和解决方案架构师理解SAP的可扩展性模型，并提供全面的决策指导。通过该指南，企业可以了解如何在SAP S/4HANA Cloud私有云版本、SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本和SAP S/4HANA与SAP业务技术平台（BTP）结合使用的环境中开发扩展。

为什么选择SAP扩展架构指南？

  • 决策支持：指南提供了一个清晰的决策框架，帮助企业在不同的技术选项中做出明智的选择。
  • 灵活性：企业可以根据自身的业务需求，选择合适的扩展模式，包括堆栈内扩展和并行扩展。
  • 未来准备：指南确保企业的扩展方案具备云就绪性和未来的弹性，帮助企业在不断变化的市场中保持竞争力。

如何使用SAP扩展架构指南？

  • 理解扩展模式：指南详细介绍了SAP S/4HANA各种版本的堆栈内扩展和SAP BTP的并行扩展模式，帮助企业根据具体需求选择合适的扩展方式。
  • 应用扩展方法论：指南基于SAP应用扩展方法论，使用其概念和术语，帮助企业开发符合核心合规要求的扩展。
  • 决策支持工具：通过指南提供的决策支持工具，企业可以更有效地分析和评估不同的扩展选项，确保选择最佳的技术方案。

扩展使用案例模式

在实际应用中，企业可能需要结合堆栈内扩展和并行扩展来满足业务需求。以下是一些常见的SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本扩展使用案例模式：

堆栈内扩展

堆栈内扩展通常需要与SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本的数据、事务或应用程序紧密耦合。以下是一些典型的堆栈内扩展特征：

  • 事务一致性：当需要同时更改SAP CDS视图和字段的数据以及自定义表和字段的数据时，事务一致性是必需的。
  • 高数据读取量：需要复杂的SQL查询和连接来读取高数据量。
  • 频繁访问和更改数据：需要频繁访问和更改SAP CDS视图和字段的数据。

实例 - 某制造企业的预付款申请流程： 客户需求是按采购订单申请预付款，并打印请款单后同步至OA进行审批，OA审批通过后生成往来付款凭证，另外客户要对申请金额进行卡控。

  1. 供应商编号选择：在进行预付款申请时，需要选择多个供应商进行付款。通过Value Help窗口，用户可以快速选择并添加多个供应商编号，确保付款申请的准确性和效率。
  2. 计划付款时间查询：需要根据不同的付款计划时间进行查询，以便安排资金流动。用户可以通过设定时间范围，快速筛选出符合条件的付款申请单。
  3. 创建预付款申请单：需要为新供应商创建预付款申请单。用户选择供应商后，系统会弹出预付款申请单页面，用户可以填写相关信息并提交申请。
  4. 提交审批：在完成预付款申请单的填写后，点击提交审批按钮，系统会提示用户确认提交，并将申请单状态修改为审批中，等待上级审批。
  5. 打印请款单：在预付款申请单提交审批后，需要打印请款单以备存档。用户选择预付款申请单后，系统会弹出PDF预览窗口，用户可以查看并打印请款单。
  6. 删除预付款申请单：在发现预付款申请单填写错误，需要删除重新申请。用户选中状态为处理中的单据后，点击删除按钮，系统会删除该申请单。
  7. 查看申请单明细：需要查看某预付款申请单的详细信息。用户点击数据行后，系统会弹出该申请单的详细页面，显示所有相关信息，方便用户进行审核和管理。

并行扩展

并行扩展可以通过API和事件与SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本解耦。以下是一些并行扩展的特征：

  • 松耦合：通过API和事件与SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本解耦。
  • 灵活性：可以独立于SAP S/4HANA Cloud公有云版本进行开发和部署。

实例 - 某制造企业的采购申请流程： 客户需求是将采购申请同步至钉钉OA进行审批，并在审批通过后生成相关凭证。此外，客户需要对采购申请的状态进行实时监控。

  1. 采购申请同步：为了能够将采购申请同步到钉钉OA，必须在SAP中定期维护钉钉部门ID和人员ID。需要同步的采购申请类型包括Z1、Z2、Z3、Z4、Z5和Z6。
  2. 采购申请审批状态同步：未打删除标记的采购申请行项目会被同步。如果所有行项目都已标记为删除，则该采购申请不会同步。在钉钉OA审批过程中发生错误时，审批状态码为99。如需重新执行审批结果同步程序，请手动将审批状态按钉钉的状态重新设置。
  3. 程序和URL：文档列出了用于同步和更新采购申请数据的各种程序及其URL。
  4. 问题和决策：文档还提到了一些在集成过程中遇到的问题和做出的决策，例如取消物料主数据和供应商主数据的集成。

结论

SAP扩展架构指南为企业提供了一个强大的工具，帮助他们在复杂的技术环境中做出明智的决策。通过理解和应用该指南，企业可以实现业务流程的定制化，提升竞争力，并为未来的发展做好准备。

扩展体系结构指南基于SAP扩展架构指南（Extension Architecture Guide） 并使用其概念和术语。了解如何使用 SAP S/4HANA 和 SAP Business Technology Platform 开发精简的核心合规扩展。有关详细信息，请参阅SAP扩展架构指南（Extension Architecture Guide） 文档。

entry.title: Solumanメインストリーム終了後のEWAレポート評価について
entry.content:
2027年12月31日にSolution Managerのメインストリームが終了します。
2028年以降に出力されるEWAレポートの評価内容は、引き続きSAPの最新情報を基に評価された内容が出力されますでしょうか？
それともEWAレポートの評価内容もSAPの保守は終了となりますでしょうか？


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solumanメインストリーム終了後のEWAレポート評価について
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

ご質問に対し、下記回答させていただきます。

>2028年以降に出力されるEWAレポートの評価内容は、引き続きSAPの最新情報を基に評価された内容が出力されますでしょうか？
それともEWAレポートの評価内容もSAPの保守は終了となりますでしょうか？

＝＞SAPの最新情報を基に評価された内容が出力されます。Solmanシステムを利用できなくなる場合、管理対象システム（ABAP）はSAP側より生成できます。詳細につき、

 KBA1257308 - FAQ: Using EarlyWatch Alertに記載する下記部分をご参照ください。

2.1 Direct Transfer Setup in ABAP

ABAP systems can be directly connected to SAP Support Portal. Refer to SAP Note 207223 and SAP Note 91488.

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。
SAPサポート

entry.title: Calculation using Scripting in Data Action
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm facing an issue while calculating the Revenue for North America. The advanced planning is being executed using scripting. Below is the logic I implemented in the data action script; however, the resulting values are incorrect.

Interestingly, when I apply the same formula directly in the account dimension table, the values appear accurately. The issue arises specifically when calculating Revenue NA through scripting, as it involves multiplying units by the lookup price.

Below are the original formulas applied in the dimension table, which yield correct results.

DRV0050 = Stored Price
DRV0020 = Units
DRV0055 Lookup Price (NA) = LOOKUP([DRV0050] ,[d/ENTITY]="#" )
- Since price for each product for all US regions stored under entity #
DRV0060 Revenue (NA) = [DRV0020]*[DRV0055]
- Exception Agg SUM by PRODUCT;ENTITY;TIME
DRV0080 Revenue (EMEA APJ) = [DRV0020]*[DRV0050]
- Exception Agg SUM by PRODUCT;ENTITY;TIME

Here is the script I used to calculate the values.

DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP()
*/
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0080") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT]="DRV0050")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0003") =
//RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0050", [d/ENTITY] = "#") /0.160899
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0006") =
//RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0050", [d/ENTITY] = "#") / 1.09556
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055",[d/ENTITY] = "REG0001") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0050", [d/ENTITY] = "#")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055",[d/ENTITY] = "REG0002") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0050", [d/ENTITY] = "#")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055",[d/ENTITY] = "REG0004") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0050", [d/ENTITY] = "#")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055",[d/ENTITY] = "REG0005") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0050", [d/ENTITY] = "#")
//DELETE([d/ACCOUNT]="DRV0060")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT]="DRV0060", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055",[d/ENTITY] = "REG0001") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT]="DRV0060") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055"
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0060", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001") =
//RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001") *
// RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001")
//DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0060", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0002") =
//RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0002") *
// RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020", [d/ENTITY] = "REG0002")

DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0060") =
RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020")

please find screen shot of accurate results 

Meanwhile, a screenshot of the inaccurate results is attached below

The issue I've identified is that the script is taking units in USD. However, in the account dimension, neither currency nor scale is defined for units. I'm unsure where exactly the problem lies.

entry.title: Creating List-Style Reports in SAP Analytics Cloud Using Custom Widgets
entry.content:
In today's data-driven era, data visualization tools have become a focal point for many, thanks to their intuitive charts, dynamic interactions, and real-time data updates. However, traditional business reports have not been phased out. Due to user habits, compliance requirements, and specific scenario needs (such as printing), traditional business reports still play a crucial role in enterprises. Yet, in the practice of SAP Analytics Cloud, traditional business reporting needs are often not well met. In this blog post, I will share a custom widget (download link) developed by SAP Analytics Cloud enthusiast Linna Zhang (GitHub profile), and explain it with some common user cases to help you understand and utilize this custom widget.

Currently, the custom widget has implemented the following features:

  • Insert new rows/columns

  • Support for cell formula calculations (e.g., SUM, MAX, MIN)

  • Merge cells

  • Indentation settings

  • Alignment settings

  • Rename cells

  • Custom units, data formats, decimal places

  • Set to percentage display, cancel percentage display

  • Drag to adjust column width

  • Hide headers

Custom Table Widget - Context Menu

 

Custom Table Widget - Additional Properties Setting

 

By leveraging the above features, we can create the following reports:

 

When using this custom widget, please note the following:

  1. Any content changes within the table will not modify the underlying model. These changes are only stored within the properties of the custom widget.

  2. If the custom table widget detects any changes in the bound data structure, all settings on the widget will be reset. Therefore, when designing the table, please ensure that the bound data structure remains unchanged (i.e., all settings in the Builder Panel are finalized) before making adjustments and styling changes to the report.

  3. Formula calculations in the table do not support directly referencing cells or ranges by clicking or dragging. You must manually input cell positions or ranges. As a result, this widget is not suitable for reports requiring fine granularity or extensive data processing.

The custom table widget can, to some extent, meet the traditional reporting needs of SAP Analytics Cloud customers. To be more widely applicable, the following features could be further optimized:

  • Font and cell style settings

  • Custom conditional formatting

  • Formula calculations in the table that allow direct referencing of cells or ranges by clicking or dragging

  • Performance and stability

What other features do you think could be further optimized in this custom table widget? Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments section.

I hope my sharing about custom table widget can provide developers with some inspiration to create more powerful and demand-meeting custom widgets.

 

 

*Disclaimer: This widget is shared solely for learning and communication purposes and is not intended for production use. Before using it, please thoroughly review and understand the licenses of any third-party libraries used. By using examples from this repository, you acknowledge that you have read and agree to comply with all terms and conditions outlined in these licenses. Please use this resource responsibly, and any risks arising from its use are solely your responsibility.

If the 'About' popup of your tenant has 'HANA Cloud version', it means your tenant is already on HANA Cloud. Otherwise it's not.  HANA Cloud is the prerequisite for seamless planning. 

Currently SAP is planning to migrate customer tenants tentatively in H2 2025.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Scrypt (2NZ) Inconsistencies
entry.content:
Dear SAP-Team,

 

During testing of the Bank Guarantee Management (2NZ) test script, we have noticed some inconsistencies regarding the topic “Issue Bank Guarantee” and “Receive Bank Guarantee” that we do not fully understand. Shouldn't a customer instead of a supplier be selected in the BP role for the transaction type 100 and a supplier for the transaction type 200? As we interpret it, the points IP_ADDRESS Issue Bank Guarantee and IP_ADDRESS Receive Bank Guarantee could have been swapped in the BP role, when creating the test script.

 

 

 

Purpose Description:

 

If you request a commercial bank to issue a Bank Guarantee for your company, normally the commercial bank will require your company to either provide cash collateral or consume your company's credit line facility in the bank. And the commercial bank will also charge a small percentage of the entire guaranteed amount.

 

In this step, create a transaction for issued Bank Guarantee.

 

Procedure:

 

Transaction type: for example 100

 

Beneficiary: Search for a supplier with the BP role FLVN00 or FLVN01, for example.

 

 

 

We were especially confused by this point, as the system then selects a purchase order as the UL Transaction Type.

 

 

 

Please review and provide your valued feedback

 

Best regards,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Scrypt (2NZ) Inconsistencies
entry.content:
Please refer to the help portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/101702563f7a3874e10000000a44147b.html

----

Partner

You specify a third party as a partner in the system that issues the letters of credit or bank guarantees at your request or notifies you about a letter of credit or bank guarantee issued with you as the beneficiary (see Business Partner ).

 

For issuing bank guarantee, it means it's issued to you and give guarantee to the supplier that you have the credit to pay them.

For receiving bank guarantee, it means bank give you guarantee that the customer has the credit to pay you.

Nevertheless, we would like to be able to handle row-level security based on the user, provided by an application SSO mechanism (in our case EntraID), which is automatically siggning in the "real" user during the report execution in the web portal.
